blocks.text: DTC P0035: P2 relief valve control SOL.V. drive circuit failure

P2 relief valve control SOL.V. controls the timing of P2 relief by the PGM-FI ECU. the PGM-FI ECU improves the response of the boost pressure rise during re-acceleration immediately after deceleration and controls the reduction of boost pressure during deceleration. P2 relief valve control SOL.V. is normally CLOSE (negative pressure side). If the return signal remains CLOSE for a predetermined period of time or longer while the PGM-FI ECU is outputting the OPEN signal to the P2 relief valve control SOL.V., the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
2D/C (2 consecutive detection method), PGM-FI warning light: On
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: P2 relief valve When the return signal remains CLOSE for 1 second or longer while the OPEN signal is being output to the control SOL.V.

items.text: Ground fault in cord (ABV line) between PGM-FI ECU and P2 relief valve control SOL.V.
Disconnection of cord (ABV line) between PGM-FI ECU and P2 relief valve control SOL.V.
Internal failure of P2 relief valve control SOL.

blocks.text: Turn ON/OFF the P2 relief valve control SOL.V. in the INSPECTION menu with HDS.

If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the DTC and freeze data are stored.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
